So you've heard me talk about statistics a few times and you've heard me talk about tracking stats in your business to having a critical operation where you're actually able to unemotionally run this whole thing from the top down. That being said, what do you actually do to track the stats on each one of these employees that you have or on your outsourced organizations? Well, it first comes with identifying what each KPI or key performance indicator is for each employee or department within your organization. For example, if I have a digital marketer, I'm going to hold him accountable to the reach or to the impressions or to the clicks or to the conversions or to the whatever I have on anything that relates to a stat that is digital marketing that he can influence in his position. That being said, if I have a social media manager, I'm holding that individual accountable on the statistics that they generate for overall reach, engagement, expansion, whatever on each post, number of posts, number of messages received, things like that. Now, how am I tracking these stats? Well, for a digital marketer, I'm using things like the Facebook Ads Manager or the Insights tab for my social media person. I use a tool called Sprout Social for my social media management because it gives us analytics on all the pages that we're pushing from for all the different social channels that we push through. If you have a different social media tool that you use for tracking, comment below. I'm interested. That being said, if you use Sprout inside of your social media management organization, that's great because you and I are using the same tool so you understand some of the stats that we have inside of that tool. I have things like engagement, audience, reach, number of posts, number of messages sent and received, and I can also see the top posts in the countries that things are coming from just in case those social media guys you have performing are buying likes or something worse. That being said, my digital marketer, I have currently reporting an end of day report each day, sending me things like Facebook ads that we've done for the day, specifically with clicks, conversions, and the amount that we spent, any kind of retargeting, the same thing, anywhere that it's coming from, I get a report on it at the end of the day, all in one email that's clear, concise, and gives me the ability to track the stats on that individual team member. My project manager I have on a weekly basis, send me the amount of projects completed, also in real time, I'm able to monitor my project manager based on the fact that they're hitting deadlines or not. So same thing, there's real time statistics, and then there's weekly reporting statistics that give you that graph so you can assess the condition of the graph so you can act accordingly with the formula steps for each one of those individual conditions to get it to the next condition. That's greater. That being said, if you are having an issue tracking any kind of stats in your business, it's probably because you're not focused or don't have the team member focus that is on the statistics being held accountable. That said, you need to make them aware, hey, you are held accountable, just as, just as, you are aware that this stat, if you do these actions would go up, right? And then the person says yes, and you say perfect. So you're acknowledging that if you take these actions once again, the stat will go up, right? Yes, okay, perfect. So when the stat goes down, I'm going to hold you accountable to it going back up because that's fair because you just acknowledge that you influence that stat, right? Yes, get perfect. When the stats go up, you're going to be the one acknowledged and commemorated for it because you're the one that influence these stats, right? Right, perfect. Okay, great. That's it. That's how you hold your team accountable to stats. How you track them, though, once you get it, is get them to report it to you. If you have a project manager, get them to report it to the project manager. But make sure you actually look at the graphs. Don't just sit there and take the data in and then not say anything back to them. Get the data, get the stats, but then talk to them about it. Every single time I social media guys send me a report, I just fucking pick it apart. I'm like, dude, what is that? What is this? What is that? What are you going to do to make that better? Because commonly it's not good. So we got to fire them. You see what I mean? But if it is good, and if it is somebody who has consistent stats that are going up, you're going to look at it and say, hey, you're doing a great job. You're crushing it. And you're going to know that you have influence in that department and you'll be able to add more clients or more results or more attention to that department to expand it. You see what I mean? You have to be able to identify just by getting your people to report on the stats in order to track it to in order to hold these people accountable to it. It's a simple way to do it. Just whatever once again, native analytics tool comes with whatever action that they're solving, you're getting a report. Super simple. Anyway, this has been how to track stats in phase number seven of the team building and execution phase. I'll see you inside that next video.
